question.label: Friction scan
question.name: What's been slower or harder than it should have been?
question.description: Invites them to name blockers without framing it as complaint.

answer: The last stretch shipped, but it was heavier than I made it sound.
note: She says the stretch was heavier than she let on, which signals hidden friction and some muted investment rather than clean momentum.

question.label: Heavier than said
question.name: When you say it was 'heavier' — what was making it heavy?
question.description: Drills into Lin's own phrasing to surface the concrete source of load or friction.

answer: Late nights came from carrying decision bottlenecks and final reviews.
note: Lin named concrete drag factors — carrying decision bottlenecks and final reviews — which signals heavy, load-bearing work reducing engagement.

question.label: Decision bottlenecks
question.name: Decision bottlenecks — where are they stacking up most right now?
question.description: Drills into the specific bottleneck Lin named to locate the main source of current friction.

answer: Some of that work could have moved to the team sooner.
note: She acknowledges work was held too long instead of moving to the team sooner, a mild negative engagement signal around ownership distribution; [THREAD-DEFERRED] delegation thread opened but drill cap forces stage advance.

question.label: Opening pace check
question.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?
question.description: Opener that invites a real answer rather than 'fine'. Reads wellbeing directly.

answer: I am proud of the outcomes, but the pace should not be normal.
note: Says the pace is unsustainably high despite pride in the outcomes — strong wellbeing risk with mild positive investment in the work.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: proud outcomes, pace — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: I still do not get enough substantive feedback on key decisions.
note: Names a specific absence — substantive feedback on key decisions — which signals strain and mild disengagement; also suggests a clarity issue worth probing next.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: still enough substantive — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: I want clearer ownership boundaries so I am not default escalation.
note: Names a specific missing condition — clearer ownership boundaries so Lin is not the default escalation — signalling sustained strain, reduced ownership quality, and lack of growth-support; also reveals a clarity issue worth probing next.

question.label: Priority clarity
question.name: What are you actually focused on this week, and why those things?
question.description: Surfaces whether priorities are crisp or vague; whether they match what you thought.

answer: Next I want to hand off one stream end-to-end and stay out of the middle.
note: Names a specific near-term priority and direction of travel — handing off one stream end-to-end to reduce middle-layer involvement — which is strong clarity and growth signal. [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'hand off one stream end-to-end' would normally merit a drill, but wind-down requires moving to the closer stage.

question.label: Steadier rhythm
question.name: What would make the next two weeks feel more sustainable?
question.description: Closes on concrete changes that would reduce load or sharpen focus.

answer: If we reset scope now, the next month is sustainable.
note: Resetting scope is named as the condition for a sustainable next month, signaling a concrete path to improved sustainability and clearer priorities.

question.label: Delegation boundary
question.name: What work are you still holding that someone else could own?
question.description: Tests delegation boundaries and whether senior-level work is being distributed well.

answer: I want to focus first on unblocking the one dependency that is slowing the team the most this week.
note: She pivots from delegation to a concrete priority — unblocking one dependency — which shows current focus but only weak signal that she is offloading work.
